title: A Coat
content:
I Made my song a coat
Covered with embroideries
Out of old mythologies
From heel to throat;
But the fools caught it,
Wore it in the world�s eyes
As though they�d wrought it.
Song, let them take it
For there�s more enterprise
In walking naked.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Butler Yeats, titled "A Coat", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
